steps.title: Use the payload ratio
steps.content: Payload capacity depends on holding force divided by weight, with a higher ratio meaning greater capacity. The text already says the Ultra-Fast Robot Hand has more than four times the Yale Model T's holding force. To support Meng's conclusion, the graph must show that Ultra-Fast is not much heavier than Yale.

steps.title: Compare the weights
steps.content: D provides the needed graph evidence: the Ultra-Fast Robot Hand weighs only slightly more than the Yale Model T, so its much larger holding force gives it the higher force-to-weight ratio. A and B give broad similarities rather than the useful comparison. C discusses the Permanent Magnet Hand and a holding-force claim that is not graph data. The answer is D.
